housing.summary: Berkeley has a well-documented housing crisis that affects students significantly, with off-campus options being expensive and sometimes unsafe. One apartment complex (Piedmont Commons) received a scathing warning about security failures, pest infestations, and negligent management. On-campus housing is competitive and limited, and a NIMBY lawsuit nearly cut freshman enrollment by 3,000 due to neighborhood opposition to university expansion.
key_quotes: Management let our front door go broken for an entire fall semester. The result: break-ins, stolen laptops, and homeless individuals stripping down in common areas
Rats are multiplying and evolving into enrolling at Berkeley and starting their own business clubs
Looking for a single apartment walkable distance from campus, no roommate... price less than 1700

social_life.summary: Social life at Berkeley gets mixed reviews — students who find their community love it, but commuters and transfer students often struggle to integrate. The campus has parties and Greek life mentioned positively, but the intense academic culture can make socializing difficult. Transfers in particular report feeling isolated and looked down upon by some peers.
key_quotes: I came into Cal as an engineering major... I realized how comfortable it felt being there
good parties (Kappa Kappa Gamma… those who know)
Just finished my first semester at UC Berkeley as a Spring Transfer and came back home with no job, no internship, no extracurriculars... feeling pretty lonely right now

dining.summary: Dining at Berkeley is consistently described as poor, with the dining halls receiving notably bad reviews. The Golden Bear Cafe is called out as a relative bright spot for sandwiches. Students seem to rely heavily on off-campus food options given the campus's urban location.
key_quotes: Berkeley has some p trash dining hall food but Golden Bear Cafe has some good sandwiches
I'd heard so much about the terrible culture/food/dorms/atmosphere/everything
I came in terrified — I'd heard so much about the terrible... food

mental_health.summary: Mental health pressures at Berkeley are real and frequently mentioned — the academic intensity, competitive culture, and imposter syndrome hit students hard, especially first-years and transfers. Students from less-prepared educational backgrounds report being blindsided by the rigor. The transition period, particularly for transfers and out-of-state students, is described as extremely difficult.
key_quotes: Just finished my first semester at UC Berkeley as a Spring Transfer and came back home with no job, no internship, no extracurriculars, family fighting... How do I navigate this doom of anxiety
I came into cal as an engineering major... the rigor here wasn't something that i was used to
It's not always easy- it's definitely been a roller coaster ride

financial_aid.summary: Financial aid concerns are prominent in Berkeley discussions, with many students making major life decisions based on cost. Some students turn down Berkeley entirely due to finances, while others note that Berkeley's aid packages can sometimes make it cheaper than expected. The high cost of living in the Berkeley/Bay Area compounds tuition costs significantly.
key_quotes: I was accepted to UC Berkeley and UC Irvine... with the financial costs that my parents stated, that was enough to dissuade me
What was the financial aid package? For most people the bigger name schools often cost less out of pocket because they give so much more aid
i'll most likely need to borrow from berkeley's student services since i can't afford a laptop on my own right now

academics.summary: Academics at Berkeley are intense and highly respected, but grade deflation and brutal curves are recurring complaints, especially in CS and engineering. Students describe being unprepared for the difficulty jump, and course staff responsiveness is sometimes poor. The CS department in particular is noted for manipulative grading bins and a sink-or-swim mentality.
key_quotes: Seems like they changed the course drastically this sem and refused to acknowledge they fucked up/change the bins
I took 61a, 16a, math 53 and 54, physics 7a and 7b... my technical gpa is a 2.78
I pick up so many additional skills from the UC curriculum... Prestige means harder classes and better preparation

administration.summary: Berkeley's administration draws significant criticism for being unresponsive and bureaucratically frustrating. Students report professors ghosting them on grades, unresolved grade disputes, and slow institutional responses to urgent issues. The university also faced controversy for sharing student names with the Trump administration and for mishandling antisemitism investigations.
key_quotes: He failed me... I emailed asking for a grade breakdown and he said he's out for summer so can't be of any help
course staff have basically ghosted us... This is actually evil like can't they at least tell us the passing threshold
UC Berkeley shares 160 names with Trump administration in 'McCarthy era' move

location_and_campus.summary: The campus itself is widely considered beautiful and inspiring, with access to San Francisco, Silicon Valley, and nature being major draws. However, the surrounding city of Berkeley has real safety concerns that students must navigate, and the urban environment means crime is a genuine consideration. The location near the Bay Area tech ecosystem is frequently cited as a major asset.
key_quotes: perfect location near Silicon Valley, San Francisco, and nature; perfect weather — nice, sunny, mild; perfect campus
It depends on what you are comfortable with... There is little crime [at UCLA's location] vs Berkeley which is nestled in a city
I realized how comfortable it felt being there

career_outcomes.summary: Career outcomes, especially in tech and CS, are considered excellent given Berkeley's proximity to Silicon Valley and its reputation as the top public CS program in the world. The name carries significant weight with employers, and connections to the Bay Area tech industry are a major selling point. Non-CS majors may find the prestige benefit less pronounced.
key_quotes: There's this girl on LinkedIn who wrote this full blog post on how she got rejected from ivys... She got a scholarship in CS to UC Berkeley
UC Berkeley is regarded as one of the top schools in the world
the name gets you hired, but it doesn't keep you employed

value_for_money.summary: For in-state students, Berkeley offers exceptional value given its world-class reputation and resources. Out-of-state and international students face a much harder calculus, with high tuition compounded by the expensive Bay Area cost of living. Several students explicitly chose cheaper alternatives despite Berkeley's prestige, and the trade-off is genuinely debated.
key_quotes: I was accepted to UC Berkeley... However, with the financial costs that my parents stated, that was enough to dissuade me
Is it really worth spending over $200k on an undergraduate degree at these schools as opposed to a state school?
In 2017, I picked UCSD over UC Berkeley for my Bioengineering undergrad because UCSD gave me basically a full-ride scholarship

overall_vibe.summary: Berkeley has a high-pressure, intellectually intense vibe that some students thrive in and others find crushing. Students who find their community and can handle the academic rigor often describe loving it deeply, while those who struggle with the competition, cost, or urban environment can find it miserable. The school attracts ambitious, motivated students but also fosters notable elitism, particularly in CS.
key_quotes: I love it here!! Cal is a beautiful, incredible, inspiring place - I have learned and grown so much here. It's not always easy
one of the most common things that I notice is how elitist some of my peers can be towards non-CS majors as well as transfer students
I always wondered why reddit made it seem like hell on earth

red_flags.summary: Several concrete red flags emerge: documented safety and management failures in off-campus housing, a CS department with reportedly manipulative grading practices, professors who are unresponsive to grade disputes, the university sharing student data with federal authorities, and a pervasive elitist culture that punishes struggling students. The housing crisis and city safety are persistent structural problems with no easy fix.
key_quotes: DO NOT SIGN A LEASE AT PIEDMONT COMMONS — break-ins, stolen laptops, and homeless individuals stripping down in common areas
Why even bother making it so hard? The CS loophole has been closed. They can reject whomever they want from the CS major now regardless of their GPA
UC Berkeley shares 160 names with Trump administration in 'McCarthy era' move... If UC Berkeley is gonna do this, what's stopping others from capitulating too?

hidden_gems.summary: Berkeley's transfer pathway from California community colleges is a genuine and well-supported route to a world-class degree, with strong acceptance rates compared to freshman admission. The Regents scholarship is mentioned as a transformative financial opportunity. The Bay Area location provides unparalleled access to tech internships, startups, and research opportunities that students at peer schools simply don't have.
key_quotes: I chose Cal mostly because of Regents and came terrified... I LOVE IT here!!
UC Berkeley and UCLA are definitely universities that will accept people like you and I [transfer students with 4.0s from CCC]
Skydeck internship is probably the method [for transfers to get ahead quickly]
